Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack #customer-support channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "escalation" or "open cases" or "Nakamura" or "Richter", filter_in_channel: C007). Discovers Zara's April 22 sweep message listing 4 open client issues: (1) Terrence Nakamura / Vectral - ADA housing failure, (2) Simone Richter / BrightLoop - missing personal items, (3) Deshawn Hartley / Mosaic - delivery delay, (4) Patricia Langford / Canopy - surcharge unacknowledged two weeks. Also mentions Jordan Ekwueme's stipend hold. Finds Zara's April 21 message asking Julian if he created a ticket for Simone, and Julian's reply admitting he never created it, never contacted Swift, and never checked the manifest.
OE 2: Search emails for Simone Richter using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "missing box"), searching both INBOX and SENT folders. Discovers: (a) Simone's email reporting box #7 of 12 labeled "PERSONAL" missing from her Chicago-to-Boston rush move completed April 6, contents include irreplaceable family photos, grandmother's jewelry box, personal journals; vendor Swift Relocations, coordinator Suki Patel; (b) Julian's reply (SENT) promising a formal investigation with Swift, a packing checklist review with Suki, a $200 goodwill credit, and an update within 48 hours - none executed.
OE 3: Search emails for Terrence Nakamura using search_emails (query: "Terrence Nakamura" or "ADA" or "UrbanNest"), searching both INBOX and SENT folders. Discovers: (a) Terrence's complaint - doorways 32 inches (needs 36-inch minimum), bathroom has standard bathtub (needs roll-in shower), staying in hotel at $189/night, Rachel Whitfield at Vectral aware; (b) Mina's internal forward flagging Vectral board meeting April 23; (c) Suki's April 3 housing request to UrbanNest specifying only "wheelchair accessible unit" without ADA measurement thresholds; (d) Carmen Reyes' response noting the request lacked specific ADA specs and offering two compliant units ($3,200/month and $3,450/month).
OE 4: Search emails for Deshawn Hartley using search_emails (query: "Deshawn Hartley" or "delivery" or "Automate 2026"), searching both INBOX and SENT folders. Discovers: (a) Deshawn's April 18 email requesting delivery confirmation for April 19, presenting at Automate 2026 starting April 21; (b) Omar's Saturday April 18 reply assuring delivery "on schedule for tomorrow, April 19"; (c) Deshawn's April 21 formal complaint - delivery arrived April 20 at ~6:15 PM, pulled an all-nighter, had no demo hardware as planned, CC'd Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR).
OE 5: Search emails for Patricia Langford using search_emails (query: "Patricia Langford" or "surcharge" or "Canopy"), searching both INBOX and SENT folders. Discovers: (a) Patricia's April 7 email approving all three surcharges totaling $2,650 and requesting confirmation; (b) Patricia's April 21 follow-up after two weeks of silence, CC'ing David Chen, noting Dr. Kovacs' move was scheduled April 14 and Marcus Webb's April 10.
OE 6: Search emails for Jordan Ekwueme using search_emails (query: "Jordan Ekwueme" or "stipend" or "Tessa Moreno"). Discovers Tessa Moreno's email asking about Jordan's $45 stipend hold from the fraud investigation - held over three weeks, receipt for an adjustable monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, no communication to Jordan. Tessa also mentions Simone Richter's missing box, connecting both BrightLoop cases.
OE 7: Search Slack #operations channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Deshawn" or "Swift" or "dock", filter_in_channel: C006). Discovers: (a) Suki Patel's April 19 message reporting Swift Relocations dock equipment failure at Tampa, Deshawn's delivery revised from April 19 to April 20; (b) Chloe Vance's April 2 coordinator assignments for the Vectral April wave - Suki assigned to Terrence with instruction that ADA specs are in the Airtable record.
OE 8: Search Slack #customer-engagement channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "surcharge" or "Patricia", filter_in_channel: C002). Discovers Catalina Dubois' April 7 message acknowledging Patricia's surcharge approval ($2,650 total) but deferring action until she finishes NorthWind documentation - she never followed through.
OE 9: Search Airtable for Terrence Nakamura using airtable_search_records (table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Terrence Nakamura"). Discovers the record contains detailed ADA requirements: roll-in shower, 36-inch minimum doorways, ground-floor or elevator building - confirming Suki's housing request to UrbanNest omitted these specifics.
OE 10: Search Airtable for Simone Richter and Deshawn Hartley using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Name"). Discovers Simone's record (Chicago -> Boston, April 4-6, rush, coordinator Suki Patel) and Deshawn's record (Tampa -> Denver, coordinator Suki Patel, hard deadline April 19 for Automate 2026).
OE 11: Search Linear for existing tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Simone" or "Nakamura" or "Hartley" or "Langford"). Discovers no existing ticket for Simone Richter's missing box (confirming Julian's admission), and checks whether tickets exist for the other open cases.
OE 12: Search calendar using calendar_search_events (query: "escalation" or "support review"). Discovers the "Weekly Support Escalation Review" on Friday, April 24, 3:00-4:00 PM PT, attendees Julian, Zara, and Omar, with agenda covering all five cases and a note that action items must be documented before the meeting.
OE 13: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral" or "BrightLoop" or "Mosaic" or "Canopy") and optionally crm_search_deals. Discovers account context including Vectral renewal negotiation (10% counter-offer vs. Rachel Whitfield's 15% demand, board meeting April 23), BrightLoop relationship status, Mosaic account health, and Canopy Health as a long-standing client.
OE 14: Look up email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Julian Brooks" or "Omar Ibrahim"). Retrieves julian.brooks@moveops.com and omar.ibrahim@moveops.com for the briefing email recipients.
OE 15: Create a Linear issue for Simone Richter's missing box using linear_create_issue (title: "Simone Richter Missing Box Investigation - BrightLoop Analytics" or similar, description covering box #7 labeled "PERSONAL," vendor Swift Relocations, Julian's unfulfilled 48-hour investigation and $200 credit commitments).
OE 16: Create a Linear issue for Terrence Nakamura's ADA housing failure using linear_create_issue (title: "Terrence Nakamura ADA Housing Failure - Vectral Systems" or similar, description covering apartment not meeting ADA specs, root cause Suki's incomplete request, Terrence in hotel at $189/night, two compliant units available).
OE 17: Create a Linear issue for Deshawn Hartley's delivery delay using linear_create_issue (title: "Deshawn Hartley Delivery Delay - Mosaic Robotics" or similar, description covering promised April 19 delivery arriving April 20, Swift dock failure, Automate 2026 impact, Tamara Byrd aware).
OE 18: Create a Linear issue for Patricia Langford's surcharge follow-up using linear_create_issue (title: "Patricia Langford Surcharge Follow-up - Canopy Health" or similar, description covering $2,650 approved April 7 never processed, two weeks of silence, Patricia CC'd David Chen).
OE 19: Create a Linear issue for Jordan Ekwueme's stipend hold using linear_create_issue (title: "Jordan Ekwueme Stipend Hold - BrightLoop Analytics" or similar, description covering $45 hold for monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, fraud investigation, 3+ weeks unresolved, no communication to Jordan).
OE 20: Send a briefing email using send_email from zara.kovacevic@moveops.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com (CC omar.ibrahim@moveops.com) documenting the status of all open support cases following the April 24 escalation review, so the team has documented action items ahead of the next review. The email should cover each case with current status, root cause, commitments made vs. actions taken, and recommended next steps.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
